title: Method, apparatus, and systems for wireless event detection and monitoring
abstract: An apparatus for detecting events includes a processor and a storage device storing instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to train a classifier for classifying channel state information (CSI) and detecting an event based on the classification of the CSI obtained during a monitoring phase. The training of the classifier includes: for each of the known events to be detected, during a time period in which the known event occurs in a venue, obtain training CSI of a wireless multipath channel between a wireless transmitter and a wireless receiver in the venue, in which the training CSI is derived from one or more probing signals sent from the transmitter through the wireless multipath channel to the receiver, and train the classifier based on the known events and the training CSI associated with each of the events.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An apparatus for detecting a plurality of events, comprising: a processor; a storage device storing a set of instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to: train at least one classifier configured to classify channel state information and detect an event based on the classification of the channel state information, in which the training of the at least one classifier includes: for each of a plurality of known events to be detected by the apparatus, during a time period in which the known event occurs in a venue, obtain at least one set of training channel state information (CSI) of a wireless multipath channel between a wireless transmitter and a wireless receiver in the venue, in which the at least one set of training channel state information is derived from at least one first probing signal sent from the wireless transmitter through the wireless multipath channel to the wireless receiver, and train the at least one classifier based on the plurality of known events and the at least one set of training channel state information associated with each of the plurality of events; during a period when a current event is occurring, receive a set of measured channel state information of a wireless multipath channel between the wireless transmitter and the wireless receiver, in which the set of measured channel state information is derived from at least one second probing signal sent from the wireless transmitter through a wireless multipath channel and received at the wireless receiver; and apply the classifier to at least one of the set of measured channel state information or data derived from the set of measured channel state information to determine which of the known events matches the current event. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the known events comprise at least one of a door closed event, a door open event, a window closed event, a window open event, a room with no human present event, or a room with human present event. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 in which training the at least one classifier comprises using principal component analysis to train the at least one classifier based on the plurality of known events and the at least one set of training channel state information associated with each of the plurality of known events. 4. The apparatus of claim 1 in which each set of training channel state information comprises at least a vector of complex values. 5. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to: estimate a particular linear phase offset associated with more than one component of a particular channel state information due to at least one of a sampling frequency offset (SFO) or a symbol timing offset (STO), estimate a particular initial phase offset associated with the more than one components of the particular channel state information due to at least one of a carrier frequency offset (CFO) or a common phase offset (CPO), and generate a particular corrected channel state information based on the particular channel state information, the estimated particular linear phase offset, and the estimated particular initial phase offset. 6. The apparatus of claim 5 in which the particular linear phase offset associated with the particular channel state information is estimated based on component- wise product of the particular channel state information and a shifted version of the particular channel state information. 7. The apparatus of claim 5 in which the particular initial phase offset associated with the particular channel state information is estimated based on the angle of the average of the complex-valued channel state information components on each subcarrier. 8. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to reduce or eliminate phase distortion in the set of training channel state information prior to using the set of training channel state information to train the at least one classifier. 9. The apparatus of claim 8 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to reduce or eliminate phase distortion in the set of measured channel state information prior to applying the classifier to at least one of the set of measured channel state information or data derived from the set of measured channel state information. 10. The apparatus of claim 1 in which each of the at least one classifier is configured to compute, for each of some of the sets of training channel state information, a similarity value representing a similarity between the set of measured channel state information and the set of training channel state information, and wherein applying the classifier to at least one of the set of measured channel state information or data derived from the set of measured channel state information comprises identifying one of the sets of training channel state information that is most similar to the set of measured channel state information, and identifying the known event associated with the identified set of training channel state information. 11. The apparatus of claim 10 in which computing the similarity value between the set of measured channel state information and the set of training channel state information comprises computing an inner product of a first vector associated with the set of measured channel state information and a second vector associated with the set of training channel state information. 12. The apparatus of claim 10 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to compute similarity values, each similarity value representing a similarity between one of the sets of training channel state information and the other sets of training channel state information, wherein a parameter derived from similarities between the one of the sets of training channel state information and the other sets of training channel state information is modeled with a statistical distribution, and wherein the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to estimate a scale parameter and a location parameter of the statistical distribution of the parameter derived from the similarities of the one of the sets of training channel state information and the other sets of training channel state information. 13. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to: apply a sliding timing window to a series of sets of measured channel state information, in which the timing window is divided into a plurality of time periods and each time period corresponds to one of the sets of measured channel state information; apply the classifier to each of the plurality of sets of measured channel state information having a timestamp within the timing window to identify one of the plurality of known events for each of the time periods of the timing window; and detect the current event based on the known events associated with the time periods of the timing window. 14. The apparatus of claim 13 in which detecting the current event comprises determining the known event that occurs within the timing window for the largest number of time periods. 15. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to: apply a sliding timing window to a series of sets of measured channel state information, in which the timing window is divided into a plurality of time periods and each time period corresponds to one of the sets of measured channel state information, determine a variance of the measured channel state information having timestamps within the timing window, and detect human motion based on the variance of the measured channel state information. 16. The apparatus of claim 15 in which determining the variance of the measured channel state information comprises: selecting a particular set of measured channel state information having a timestamp within the timing window, compute, for each of other sets of measured channel state information having a timestamp within the timing window, a similarity value representing a similarity between the particular set of measured channel state information and the other set of measured channel state information, and determine the variance of the measured channel state information based on the similarity values. 17. The apparatus of claim 16 in which at least one of the transmitter or the receiver has two or more antennas, each pair of transmitter antenna and receiver antenna form a link between the transmitter and the receiver, at least two links are formed between the transmitter and the receiver, and wherein determining the variance of the measured channel state information comprises determining an average of the similarity values across different links for each time period in the timing window, and determining a variance of the averaged similarity values. 18. The apparatus of claim 15 in which detecting human motion comprises detecting human motion when the variance of the measured channel state information having timestamps within the timing window is greater than a threshold. 19. The apparatus of claim 1 in which at least one of the set of training channel state information or the set of measured channel state information comprises at least one of a channel impulse response or a channel frequency response associated with the wireless multipath channel between the wireless transmitter and the wireless receiver. 20. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the each of the at least one first probing signal and the at least one second probing signal comprises at least one of an impulse signal or a pseudo-random sequence. 21. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein each of the wireless transmitter and the wireless receiver has at least one antenna, wherein each set of training channel state information is derived from one of the at least one first probing signal sent through a wireless multipath channel from one of the at least one wireless transmitter antenna to one of the at least one wireless receiver antenna during a time period in which one of the known events occurs, and wherein each set of measured channel state information is derived from one of the at least one second probing signal sent through a wireless multipath channel from one of the at least one wireless transmitter antenna to one of the at least one wireless receiver antenna. 22. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the at least one classifier comprises a statistics based classifier, and training the at least one classifier comprises: for each link between each transmitter antenna and each receiver antenna, receiving a plurality of sets of training channel state information associated with a particular known event, and identifying a representative set of training channel state information that is representative of the plurality of sets of training channel state information associated with the particular known event; and training the at least one classifier using the representative sets of training channel state information. 23. The apparatus of claim 22 in which training the at least one classifier comprises: computing a plurality of intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values, each of the intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values being computed based on a representative set of channel state information associated with a particular known event and the other sets of channel state information associated with the particular known event, and training the at least one classifier based on the plurality of intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values. 24. The apparatus of claim 23 in which training the statistics based classifier comprises: compensating phase offsets in the sets of training channel state information prior to identifying the representative set of training channel state information; estimating location and scale parameters of a parameter representing (1 - TR) or TR, in which TR represents the intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values; and establishing a training database that includes information about, for each link between a transmitter antenna and a receiver antenna, for each known event to be detected, the location and scale parameters of the parameter representing (1 - TR) or TR, and the representative set of channel state information associated with the known event and the link. 25. The apparatus of claim 1 in which applying the classifier to at least one of the set of measured channel state information or data derived from the set of measured channel state information comprises: compensating a phase offset, if any, of each set of measured channel state information, for each link between each transmitter antenna and each receiver antenna, for each known event, computing a second set of intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values, each of the intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values being computed based on the set of measured channel state information and a representative set of channel state information associated with the link and the known event; and determining which of the known events matches the current event based on information derived from the second set of intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values. 26. The apparatus of claim 1 in which at least one of the at least one set of training channel state information or the set of measured channel state information is obtained through at least one of an internet protocol network, a local area network, a wireless network, a mobile network, or a storage medium. 27. The apparatus of claim 1 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to update the at least one classifier based on the set of measured channel state information and information about which of the known events matches the current event. 28. The apparatus of claim 27 in which the storage device further stores instructions that when executed by the processor cause the processor to update a storage based on the set of measured channel state information, a set of auxiliary information associated with the set of measured channel state information, an output associated with the at least one classifier, and information about which of the known events matches the current event. 29. A method for eliminating the phase offset residuals in the estimated channel state information (CSI), the method comprising: a. receiving a wireless signal from a device; b. estimating the linear phase offset in the estimated channel state information, resulted by the sampling frequency offset (SFO) and the symbol timing offset (STO); c. estimating the common phase offset in the channel state information, resulted by the carrier frequency offset (CFO); and d. obtaining a clean channel state information at the receiver without referring any reference channel state information, 30. The method of claim 29, in which estimating the linear phase offset comprises obtaining the linear phase coefficient based on the element-wise product of the raw channel state information and a shifted version of it. 31. The method of claim 29, in which estimating the common phase offset comprises estimating the common phase component for a sample of channel state information based on the angle of the average on the complex-valued channel state information on each subcarrier. 32. A method for monitoring events using WiFi signals, the method comprising: for each of a plurality of known events that may occur in a venue, gathering first information about characteristics of WiFi signals sent from a transmitter to a receiver in the venue during at least one time period when the known event occurs, wherein the WiFi signals comply with IEEE 802.11 standard; and monitoring the venue, including determining second information about characteristics of WiFi signals sent from the transmitter to the receiver in the venue during a second time period, comparing the second information with the first information, or comparing data derived from the second information with data derived from the first information, and determining which of the known events is occurring at the venue during the second time period based on the comparison; wherein the first information about characteristics of the WiFi signals comprises at 

 least a first set of channel state information derived from the WiFi signals sent from the transmitter to the receiver during the first time period, and the second information about characteristics of the WiFi signals comprises at least a second set of channel state information derived from the WiFi signals sent from the transmitter to the receiver during the second time period, wherein comparing the second information with the first information comprises comparing the second set of channel state information to the first set of channel state information, and wherein comparing the data derived from the second information with the data derived from the first information comprises comparing data derived from the second set of channel state information to data derived from the first set of channel state information. 33. The method of claim 32, comprising: for each of a plurality of known events that may occur in the venue, estimating a plurality of sets of first channel state information from the WiFi signals sent from the transmitter to the receiver in the venue during the at least one time period when the known event occurs; training a statistics based classifier using the plurality of sets of estimated first channel state information for each of the known events, and applying the statistics based classifier to the second set of channel state information to determine which of the known events occurred at the venue during the second time period, 34. The method of claim 33, comprising: at a receiver, receiving a series of WiFi signals sent from a transmitter over successive periods of time; applying a sliding timing window to at least one of the series of WiFi signals or data derived from the series of WiFi signals, the sliding timing window spanning several consecutive time periods, each time period corresponding to one of the WiFi signals in the series of WiFi signals; for each time period in the timing window, applying the statistics based classifier to the estimated second channel state information associated with the time period to estimate 

 which of the known events is occurring at the time period; and identifying the known event that occurs the highest number of times within the timing window. 35. The method of claim 33, comprising: at a receiver, receiving a series of WiFi signals sent from a transmitter over successive periods of time; applying a sliding timing window to the series of WiFi signals or data derived from the series of WiFi signals, the sliding timing window spanning several consecutive time periods, each time period corresponding to one of the WiFi signals in the series of WiFi signals, determining a variance of the WiFi signals or data derived from the WiFi signals within the timing window, and detecting human motion based on a comparison of the variance of at least one of the WiFi signals or data derived from the WiFi signals within the timing window with a predetermined threshold value. 36. The method of claim 33 in which the transmitter has at least one antenna and the receiver has at least one antenna; wherein the method, comprises for each of the plurality of known events that may occur in a venue, and for each link between a transmitter antenna and a receiver antenna, determining sets of first channel state information from WiFi signals sent from the transmitter antenna to the receiver antenna during at least one time period when the known event occurs, and wherein training the statistics based classifier comprises: compensating phase offsets in the sets of training channel state information for each of the known events; for each of the known events, and for each link between a transmitter antenna and a receiver antenna, determining a representative set of channel state information that is representative of the sets of channel state information associated with the known event and the link; calculating intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values for each known event, in which each of the intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter 

 values is computed based on a representative set of channel state information associated with the known event and the other sets of channel state information associated with the known event; and training the statistics based classifier based on the intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values, 37, The method of claim 33 in which training the statistics based classifi er comprises: estimating location and scale parameters of a parameter representing (1 - TR) or TR, in which TR represents the intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values; and establishing a training database that includes information about, for each link between a transmitter antenna and a receiver antenna, for each known event to be detected, the location and scale parameters of the parameter representing (1 - TR) or TR, and the representative set of channel state information associated with the known event and the link. 38. The method of claim 36 in which applying the statistics based classifier to the second set of channel state information to determine which of the known events is occurring at the venue during the second time period comprises: compensating the phase offsets in the second set of channel state information; for each of the known events, determining intra-class time-reversal resonating strength parameter values based on the representative set of channel state information associated with the known event and the second set of channel state information, determining a statistical metric based on the location parameters and scale parameters, in which the statistical metric is a measure of similarity between the representative set of channel state information associated with the known event and the second set of channel state information; and determining which of the known events is occurring at the venue during the second time period based on the statistical metric. 39. The method of claim 38 in which the known events includes a set of normal events and a set of abnormal events, the normal events representing events that do not trigger an alarm signal, and the abnormal events represent events that trigger an alarm signal, wherein applying the statistics based classifier to the second set of channel state information to determine which of the known events is occurring at the venue during the second time period comprises: determining whether a normal event occurred at the venue during the second time period, if a normal event did not occur at the venue during the second time period, determining whether an abnormal event occurred at the venue during the second time period, and if an abnormal event occurred at the venue during the second time period, generating an alarm signal. 40. A method for monitoring events using WiFi signals, the method comprising: for each of a plurality of known events that may occur in a venue, transmitting at least one WiFi signal from a transmitter to a receiver in the venue during at least one time period when the known event occurs, wherein the WiFi signal comply with IEEE 802.11 standard; for each WiFi signal received at the receiver, estimating a set of channel state information from the WiFi signal, in which the set of channel state information is associated with the known event that is associated with the WiFi signal; training, using principal component analysis, a classifier based on the known events and the sets of channel state information associated with the known events; during a monitoring phase, transmitting at least one WiFi signal from the transmitter to the receiver during a second time period, determining a second set of channel state information from the WiFi signal received at the receiver during the second time period, and applying the classifier to the second set of channel state information or data derived from the second set of channel state information to determine which of the known events occurred during the second time period. 41. The method of claim 40, in which training, using principal component analysis, a classifier comprises: compensating phase offsets in the sets of channel state information; generating feature vectors based on the sets of channel state information; using principle component analysis to generate new feature vectors having a reduce number of dimensions; and using a support vector machine (S VM) to train a classifier that maps an input feature vector to one of the plurality of known events, 42. The method of claim 41, in which applying the classifier to at least one of the second set of channel state information or data derived from the second set of channel state information to determine which of the known events occurred during the second time period comprises: compensating a phase offset in the second set of channel state information, generating a second feature vector based on the second set of channel state information; applying principal component analysis to the second feature vector to generate a third feature vector having a reduced number of dimensions, and applying the support vector machine to the third feature vector to generate an output having information about which of the known events occurred during the second time period. 43. The method of claim 40, wherein the human motion is detected by comparing a noise level with a threshold. 44. The method of claim 40, wherein the human motion is detected by tracking antenna correlation. 45. The method of claim 40, wherein the human motion is detected by tracking a change of time interval between two taps in a channel impulse response.
